title: Bridge Essay: Predictions transform memories: How expected versus unexpected events are integr

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Predictions, transform, memories:, How, expected.

**Predictions transform memories: How expected versus unexpected events are integrated or separated in memory.** — Neurosci Biobehav Rev. PMID: 37619645. DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2023.105368. Our brains constantly generate predictions about the environment based on prior knowledge. Many of the events we experience are consistent with these predictions, while others might be inconsistent with prior knowledge and thus violate our predictions. To guide future behavior, the memory system must be able to strengthen, transform, or add to existing knowledge based on the accuracy of our predictions. We synthesize recent evidence suggesting that when an event is consistent with our prediction
